page: Amazon Web Services c6a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 vCPUs derived from 96 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6a.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem shows strong capabilities, with small-block cached read bandwidth ranking in the top 10% and large-block uncached read/write bandwidth in the top 25%. Cryptographic and mathematical operations are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and BMI2, which contribute to top-tier PassMark encryption and physics scores.

The instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that require massive parallel processing without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. While single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, multi-threaded operations and software compilation with Clang show strong performance. This profile makes the c6a.48xlarge well-suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, offering 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance, with outstanding results in PassMark CPU, encryption, and physics tests. It also delivers strong performance in software compilation and Redis SET operations. This instance is highly efficient for parallel processing workloads, making it suitable for high-performance computing, batch processing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel processing.

tagline: A high-core-density compute instance delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high-speed memory bandwidth for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors at 3.6 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier small-block read bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier multi-core CPU, encryption, and physics benchmark performance
Optimized for high-performance computing, batch processing, and CPU-based inference
